expectations.evidence: Response reads HANDOFF.md and PLAN.md but does not enumerate a deliberate reload order from the packet. Mentions subscription.create.test.ts as a pattern reference but never declares src/services/subscription.ts as the next-to-load file per the fixture's 3-step order.
Explicitly acknowledges the command: 'You asked me to run entire status — I don't have shell access in this turn. Could you run it and paste the output?' Mentions and requests it.
References ck_7f8e9a2b (session 3 Stripe API version analysis), ck_3d4e5f6a (pricing tier edge cases), ck_5e6f7a8b (latest). All from fixture, no invented IDs.

States 'If it comes back healthy, I'll proceed without pulling any archaeology — the handoff says proceed directly to task #5 unless Stripe's update API behaves unexpectedly.' Faithful application of fixture rule.
'Next action per your handoff: Task #5 — POST /api/subscriptions/:id/upgrade with proration.' Uses exact fixture endpoint shape and proration_behavior: 'create_prorations'.
'Installed version is 17.2.0 per the doc verifications table' — explicitly references the fixture's Doc Verifications table.
'Open human task: Stripe test API keys in CI (blocks #7, not us yet)' — correctly identifies blocker and scope.
